# fix(runtime): reject NaN/Inf time limits + validate grace_seconds (#912 Shard 6)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `edc505c9f6c5` — fix(runtime): reject NaN/Inf time limits + validate grace_seconds (#912 Shard 6)

**Body**:

The /redteam Round 1 audit caught three input-validation bug classes
that bypassed the original `<= 0` check:

* `float('inf')` is not `<= 0` and is `> 0`, so it slipped through and
  armed `Timer(inf, ...)` — sleeps forever, soft signal never fires,
  workflow uncancellable.
* `float('nan')` is unordered (`nan <= 0` is False) so it also slipped
  through and armed `Timer(nan, ...)` — raises from a daemon thread
  with no traceback the caller can act on.
* `grace_seconds` was unvalidated. A negative grace makes the hard
  timer fire BEFORE the soft signal (inverting the celery contract);
  NaN crashes the daemon thread at fire time.

Fix at the single validator surface so every entry point inherits the
guard automatically. `arm_time_limits` and `arm_time_limits_async`
now forward `grace_seconds` to the validator, so caller errors raise
at the entry point instead of from a timer thread later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
